unlock.title: cryptessage
unlock.subtitle: Passphrase protects your vault. It is never stored.

home.title: Chats
home.empty: No contacts yet. Add someone from Contacts.

contacts.title: Contacts
contacts.addPaste: Paste visit card (JSON), armored public key block, or scan QR — not the fingerprint hex
contacts.shareUnavailable: Sharing is not available on this device or browser.
contacts.pasteQrNoCode: Clipboard image has no readable QR code.
contacts.reviewQrTooLong: Payload is too long to render as a single QR in this app; the decoded text is still shown for validation.
contacts.reviewInvalid: Not a valid visit card or key
contacts.reviewKeyValid: OpenPGP public key parses and validates.
contacts.reviewInvalidCompact: Not a valid compact visit card.
contacts.reviewInvalidCompactPaste: Expected base64 of compact visit card binary.
contacts.visitCardUsesPrefs: Format follows Settings → visit card preference.

chat.title: Chat
chat.exportForContactHint: Share this QR or armored text with %{name}. It is encrypted for their key—only they can decrypt it. You cannot paste it into “Receive” below; that area is for messages they send you.
chat.receiveOnlyIncomingHint: Use this when %{name} sent you ciphertext (encrypted for your public key). Your own outgoing QR or paste goes to them, not here.
chat.errCannotDecryptOwnOutgoing: This message is encrypted for the contact’s public key, not yours—only they can decrypt it. “Receive” is for incoming messages from this contact (encrypted for you).
chat.qrHint: Single QR fits about %{max} characters; shorten the message if the QR fails.
chat.qrBinaryHint: QR uses binary OpenPGP (CMM1 wrapper), about %{max} bytes max for this app.
chat.qrTooLarge: Ciphertext is too large for a single QR (>%{max} bytes). Shorten the message or use copy/paste.
chat.deleteChatBody: This removes %{name} from your contacts and deletes all messages in this chat. This cannot be undone.

settings.title: Settings
settings.backupHint: Backup includes contacts, messages, and keys. Keep the file and passphrase separate.
settings.publicKeyHelp: Share this armored block or your QR visit card on Contacts. The fingerprint above is only an ID — others cannot add you from it alone.
settings.visitCardFormatHint: OpenPGP is larger but interoperable; Compact v1 is ~70–90 bytes for easier QR scanning.
settings.updateAppHint: Clears the installed app cache and reloads so the latest version can load. Your vault stays on this device.

push.title: Push notifications
push.hint: Get alerts when the app is in the background. Requires HTTPS (or localhost) and a configured VAPID public key at build time.
push.enableHint: Subscribe on this device. You can turn this off anytime.
push.testHint: Shows a local notification to verify permission and the service worker.
push.payloadHint: Servers should send JSON push payloads with optional fields: title, body, url. Use "#/…" when the app is built with hash routing (e.g. GitHub Pages); otherwise use paths relative to the app, such as "./settings".
push.unsupportedDetail: Push is not available in this browser or context (use HTTPS, a supported browser, and a secure context).
push.noVapidDetail: This build has no VITE_WEB_PUSH_PUBLIC_KEY. Add your VAPID public key to the environment to enable subscription.
push.permissionDeniedDetail: Notifications are blocked for this site. Change permission in the browser address bar or site settings, then try again.
push.iosPwaHint: On iPhone and iPad, add the app to the Home Screen and open it from there for the best push support.

error.noVapid: Push is not configured (missing VAPID public key).
error.unsupported: Push notifications are not supported here.

transport.priorityHint: Higher items are tried first when sending. Offline QR is always available as fallback.
transport.panelUnsupportedHint: This transport is registered, but this version only ships UI for some channels. Use another tab or QR.
transport.settingsHint: Profiles store URLs and secrets (encrypted). New transport types register in the app when their modules load.
transport.label: Label
transport.defaultTransport: Default transport (when a contact has no custom order)
transport.invalidJson: Config must be valid JSON object.
transport.httpInboxIdLabel: HTTP REST inbox id (for this contact)
transport.httpRestDeliveredBody: Ciphertext for %{name} was posted to the server (HTTP %{status}).
transport.httpRestNoQrHint: You do not need a QR for this send unless you also want an offline copy.
transport.httpRestAwait: Network send did not complete for this attempt; use another tab or QR.
transport.httpRestReceiveHint: To receive over HTTP, set "outboxSelfKeyId" (your mailbox id on this server), optional "outboxPathTemplate" (default "/outbox/{{selfKeyId}}"), and optional "pollIntervalMs" (1000–60000, default 10000). The app polls while unlocked and persists the server cursor per profile. Set "enablePoll": false to disable automatic polling; then use the refresh button in the chat header to fetch once.

qr.visitCardBinaryMax: Visit card QR (binary mode), max payload bytes (approx.)
